note: Source URL https://www.meti.go.jp/policy/consumer/seian/denan/index.html is the canonical METI Denan page — confirmed live (HTTP 403 to scrapers but domain/path verified via search results, multiple METI documents reference it). Source is official. HOWEVER: (1) The fragment incorrectly classifies PV inverters as 'specified electrical appliances (特定電気用品)' requiring the PSE DIAMOND mark — search evidence strongly suggests PV power conditioners fall under 'non-specified electrical appliances (その他の電気用品)' and require the PSE CIRCLE mark with self-declaration, not third-party inspection by a registered body. This is a material status error. (2) JIS C 8961:2008 is an efficiency measurement method standard, not a safety standard referenced under Denan for PSE conformity; it does not govern PSE compliance. The correct Denan safety standards for PV inverters are JIS C 62109-1 and JIS C 62109-2 (via Appendix 12 of the technical standards Ministerial Ordinance), plus JIS C 8980:2020. (3) JIS C 62109-1 is correctly cited. mandatory_status.target claims 'third-party conformity assessment by a registered inspection body (登録検査機関)' — this applies to SPECIFIED products only; if PV inverters are non-specified, the obligation is self-declaration, not registered-body inspection.

Source URL https://www.jisc.go.jp/eng/index.html confirmed live and is the official JISC English homepage (verified via direct fetch: hosted on jisc.go.jp, contact email bzl-jisc@meti.go.jp, copyright JISC). JIS C 62109-1 and JIS C 62109-2 are the correct technical standards for PV inverter safety under Denan (equivalent to IEC 62109-1:2010 and IEC 62109-2:2011 respectively). The fragment notes '[UNVERIFIED — confirm JIS adoption status and year]' for JIS C 62109-2: search evidence confirms both parts are adopted as JIS under Denan Appendix 12. The gap description (CB test reports not directly usable for PSE, registered inspection body must review) is accurate. mandatory_status.target is accurate in that technical standard conformity is a statutory condition of PSE marking. The UNVERIFIED caveat in the fragment is appropriate and honest.

Source URL https://www.meti.go.jp/policy/consumer/seian/denan/index.html confirmed official (same as pse-marking-requirement row — canonical Denan page). The legal articles cited (Art. 3 事業届出, Art. 8 技術基準適合義務, Art. 10 表示義務) are the correct provisions of 電気用品安全法. The gap description (no CN-side parallel; legal responsibility shifts to the Japan-side business notifier) is accurate and well-documented. mandatory_status is correct. This row does not repeat the specified/non-specified classification error from pse-marking-requirement because it focuses on the registration obligation which applies to both categories.

Source URL https://www.jet.or.jp/products/renewable/solar/grid/index.html returns HTTP 404. The correct current URL for JET grid-connected inverter (power conditioner) certification is https://www.jet.or.jp/renewable/power_conditioner/protection/ (confirmed live via direct fetch — describes certification of grid-interconnection protection devices for PV systems, references 系統連系技術要件ガイドライン and 電気用品安全法). The English version is at https://www.jet.or.jp/en/products/protection/index.html (confirmed live). Source URL must be updated. Standards (JEAC 9701, 電気事業法) and mandatory_status ('Mandatory — required by the Electricity Business Act for grid connection') are substantively correct; JET certification is de-facto mandatory via utility interconnection application requirements. Gap description (3-6 months lead time, GB/T 19964 not recognised) is accurate.

Source URL https://www.meti.go.jp/policy/safety_security/industrial_safety/oshirase/2011/pdf/0422-1-3.pdf returns HTTP 403 to scrapers and cannot be confirmed as the authoritative 50Hz/60Hz source — the specific 2011 document path could not be verified; the document may have been superseded or moved. The underlying fact (Japan operates 50 Hz East / 60 Hz West split; dual frequency required for national sales; JEAC 9701 governs operating ranges) is correct and confirmed by multiple authoritative sources. A more stable replacement URL is the ANRE/METI page https://www.enecho.meti.go.jp/category/saving_and_new/saiene/index.html or the Japan Electric Association JEAC 9701 store (https://store.denki.or.jp/products/detail/728). standards_ok and status_ok are confirmed correct. Source URL needs updating.

Source URL https://www.jet.or.jp/products/renewable/solar/grid/index.html returns HTTP 404 (same dead URL as jppv-grid-01). Correct URL is https://www.jet.or.jp/renewable/power_conditioner/protection/ (confirmed live). Standards listed include 'JIS C 8961 [UNVERIFIED]': this is a likely error — JIS C 8961:2008 covers efficiency measurement methods for PV power conditioners, NOT anti-islanding protection. The correct standard for anti-islanding testing is JIS C 8963 (系統連系形太陽光発電システム用パワーコンディショナの単独運転検出機能の試験方法), confirmed by search results. JEAC 9701 is correctly cited for anti-islanding thresholds. mandatory_status (both active and passive methods required, verified during JET certification) is correct per JEAC 9701. Source URL must be updated; JIS C 8961 reference should be replaced with JIS C 8963.

Source URL https://www.meti.go.jp/policy/safety_security/industrial_safety/oshirase/2006/07/gazofiles/190906-guideline.pdf returns HTTP 403 and cannot be confirmed accessible. The 高調波抑制対策ガイドライン is a real METI/ANRE document but this specific 2006 URL path is likely stale. The guideline has been updated via JEAG 9702. JEAG 9702 is confirmed real and current: latest version is JEAG 9702-2023, published September 2024 by the Japan Electric Association (store.denki.or.jp), confirming the fragment's reference is valid though the cited version is outdated. A more current source would be the JEAG 9702-2023 product page at https://store.denki.or.jp/products/高調波抑制対策技術指針-第4版. Standards (高調波抑制対策ガイドライン, JIS C 61000-3-2, JEAG 9702) are all confirmed real. mandatory_status (mandatory as grid interconnection requirement) is correct. Gap description (utility harmonic flow calculation submission) is accurate. Source URL needs updating.

Source URL https://www.vcci.jp/vcci_e/overview/overview.html confirmed live — official VCCI Council English page (verified via direct fetch: VCCI Council header, EMI references, Tokyo address, membership portal). Source is official. HOWEVER: the fragment states VCCI applies to 'communication or IT-adjacent interfaces' in PV inverters and references CISPR 32 / CISPR 22. Search results confirm VCCI scope is specifically Information Technology Equipment (ITE) and multimedia equipment — PV inverters without embedded IT interfaces are typically OUTSIDE VCCI scope. The fragment's own '[UNVERIFIED applicability to PV inverters]' caveat is therefore well-placed and correct: VCCI likely does NOT apply to a standalone PV inverter without communication/IT module. The mandatory_status 'Voluntary (VCCI) + Mandatory if wireless (電波法 / MIC certification)' is correct for the wireless portion. The gap analysis regarding MIC 技術基準適合証明 for wireless modules is accurate. standard_ok set to false only for the VCCI scope uncertainty — the cited JIS C 61000 series and 電波法 references are correct.

Source URL https://www.meti.go.jp/policy/consumer/seian/denan/index.html is the canonical METI Denan page (confirmed official via search). Standards cited (電気用品安全法 Act No. 234, Cabinet Order, Ministerial Ordinance on Technical Standards) are all correct. HOWEVER: mandatory_status.target states 'pre-market notification and PSE mark required' — this is accurate. But the fragment says inverters fall under 'specified electrical appliances (特定電気用品)' OR 'other electrical appliances' — multiple search sources indicate PV power conditioners are classified as non-specified (その他の電気用品), requiring the PSE CIRCLE mark and self-declaration rather than third-party inspection. This is a significant gap/status nuance. The gap description mentions PSE diamond mark (specified) or circle mark (other) which is accurate as a general statement. Needs human verification of the exact product classification in the current 特定電気用品 list (116 items) vs the non-specified list (341 items).

Source URL https://www.jet.or.jp/products/solar/index.html resolves but leads to the JET PVm MODULE certification page (JETPVm — for PV modules, not inverters), not the grid-connected inverter (power conditioner) certification page. This is the wrong JET page for this row (which is about grid interconnection certification for inverters). The correct URL is https://www.jet.or.jp/en/products/protection/index.html (low-voltage grid-connected inverters / power conditioners, confirmed live and on-point). Standards listed (系統連系技術要件ガイドライン, JIS C 8961, JIS C 8980, JET Certification Standards) are mostly correct — JIS C 8961 is an efficiency measurement standard not a grid protection standard; JIS C 8963 is more relevant for anti-islanding. JIS C 8980 is the correct performance test standard. mandatory_status ('voluntary in law but de-facto mandatory in practice') is accurate and confirmed. Source URL must be corrected.

Source URL https://www.enecho.meti.go.jp/category/saving_and_new/saiene/kaitori/fit_fitsu.html returns HTTP 403. The URL path 'fit_fitsu' could not be confirmed as a currently valid page. However, the ANRE FIT/FIP scheme is confirmed real and actively maintained: search found multiple active enecho.meti.go.jp pages including https://www.enecho.meti.go.jp/category/saving_and_new/saiene/kaitori/surcharge.html and the 2026 FIT/FIP Guidebook PDF. A more reliable source URL would be https://www.enecho.meti.go.jp/category/saving_and_new/saiene/kaitori/surcharge.html or the main page https://www.enecho.meti.go.jp/category/saving_and_new/saiene/index.html. Standards (電気事業法, 系統連系技術要件ガイドライン, FIT/FIP Act, DENAN) are all correct. mandatory_status is accurate. Gap description (three separate compliance steps) is accurate. Source URL must be updated.

Source URL https://www.jeac.or.jp/ returned a socket connection error and could not be confirmed live. The Japan Electric Association (日本電気協会) is the confirmed publisher of JEAC 9701 (verified via search: JEAC 9701 store listing at store.denki.or.jp/products/detail/728, and the JESC approval records). The domain jeac.or.jp appears to exist but may have connectivity issues. A more stable and confirmed URL for JEAC 9701 is the Japan Electric Association webstore: https://store.denki.or.jp/products/detail/728. Standards (JEAC 9701, 電気事業法) are correct. The '[UNVERIFIED]' notes on specific clause numbers and TSO rule references are appropriate — JEAC 9701 clause numbers require paid document access. mandatory_status is correct. The 50Hz/60Hz split boundary (roughly Shizuoka/Nagano prefectures) is factually confirmed. Source URL should be updated.

Source URL https://www.meti.go.jp/policy/safety_security/industrial_safety/oshirase/2011/03/230331-1.html returns HTTP 403 and the specific document could not be confirmed. The 2011/03 path exists in the METI oshirase directory (a related file 230331-7-1.pdf was found), but the HTML page 230331-1.html could not be verified as current or accessible. A more authoritative and stable source for 単独運転検出 requirements would be the current 電気設備の技術基準の解釈 (METI technical standard interpretations) or the JEAC 9701 store page. Standards cited (JEAC 9701, 電気事業法 Art. 39, METI Ministerial Ordinance, TSO Grid Codes) are all correct in substance, though the '[UNVERIFIED]' flags on specific article numbers are appropriate. mandatory_status is correct. Gap description (Japan-specific active anti-islanding detection thresholds, FRT profiles differ from China) is accurate and confirmed. Source URL must be updated.
